stories.title: Elon Musk brutal reply to journalist’s ‘people loathe you’ remark wins internet
stories.summary: Elon Musk brutal reply to journalist’s ‘people loathe you’ remark wins internet Elon Musk’s recent interview with The Economist Editor-in-Chief has become the talk of the town and the most talked-about moment was SpaceX's savage reply to her question. The fiery exchange between Musk and Zanny Minton Beddoes stemmed from her remarks, claiming that many people loathe the 54-year-old billionaire. During the interview, Minton argued about Musk’s growing involvement in political affairs specifically outside the US, stating, “You don't even live there. ” She remarked that people felt uncomfortable with the “world’s richest man’s” involvement in politics though he does not live here.

stories.title: Police say Australian World Cup player Volpato has tested positive for cocaine
stories.summary: Democracy Dies in Darkness Soccer Cristian Volpato, who represented Australia at the World Cup, has allegedly tested positive for cocaine and also for speeding, New South Wales state police said July 24, 2026 at 3:46 a. m. EDT 8 minutes ago Australia’s Cristian Volpato arrives at the stadium ahead the World Cup round of 32 soccer match between Australia and Egypt in Arlington, Texas, near Dallas, Friday, July 3, 2026. (AP Photo/Sam Hodde) By Associated Press SYDNEY — Cristian Volpato, who represented Australia at the World Cup , has allegedly tested positive for cocaine and also for speeding, New South Wales state police said Friday.

stories.title: Alpe d’Huez twice: the epic 2026 Tour finale
stories.summary: Two stages, two completely different approaches to one of cycling’s most iconic climbs, and a spectacular finale in the battle for the podium. Stage 19: The classic climb with 21 hairpin bends Friday’s Stage 19 features the legendary ascent from Bourg-d’Oisans to Alpe d’Huez. At just under 128 kilometres, it is the shortest mass-start stage of this year’s Tour, but don’t let the distance fool you. With around 3,500 metres of climbing, the riders face a demanding day capped by one of cycling’s most famous finishes. The 16-kilometre climb, with its iconic 21 hairpin bends, is synonymous with Tour de France history.

stories.title: View Interior Photos of the 2027 Maserati Grecale Modena
stories.summary: Read the Full Story | View Exterior PhotosThe 2027 Maserati Grecale features a few tweaks to its cabin, but the model year's most notable changes happen elsewhere in the vehicle. In base-trim form, the Grecale's interior quality outpaces the gas-powered Porsche Macan's. We're not the biggest fans of that slanted touch panel for the climate controls, though. The seats, however, are marvelous. Look, ma—real buttons! The mid-level Modena trim picks up a 14-speaker Sonus Faber sound system. Keep going to check out even more pictures of the 2027 Maserati Grecale's interior.

stories.title: Ukraine ‘priority’ for Ireland during EU presidency, Taoiseach says
stories.summary: The Irish premier has said it was “very important” to go to Ukraine early in Ireland’s EU presidency as a demonstration of solidarity. Micheál Martin was speaking to reporters in the country’s capital, Kyiv , on Thursday. Asked if there was a danger the EU’s focus would drift because of the war in the Middle East, he said “we have too many wars in the world”, adding that conflict has had “a very, very negative impact” there and on the wider world. The Taoiseach said it was “very, very important to come out here early” during Ireland’s six-month presidency of the Council of the European Union to make clear to Ukraine “they are a priority in terms of the Irish presidency”.

stories.title: Aughinish Alumina investigation makes ‘no adverse findings’ against Co Limerick plant
stories.summary: An investigation into Aughinish Alumina and the company’s export of raw material to Russia has made “no adverse findings” against the Co Limerick plant, Minister for Enterprise Peter Burke has said. In a statement setting out the findings of the inquiry, Burke said there was “no information of a sufficient evidentiary level” found to confirm that alumina produced by the Russian-owned plant was later being used to supply Russian arms manufacturers making missiles and weapons for Moscow’s war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana manipulating the system, families' lawyer says
stories.summary: 6 minutes ago Olivia Ireland Family photos Bebe King, Elsie Dot Stancombe and Alice da Silva Aguiar were killed in the 29 July 2024 attack A lawyer for the families of the children murdered by Southport killer Axel Rudakubana says his transfer from prison to Broadmoor psychiatric hospital is another attempt by him to "manipulate the system". Rudakubana, 19, was given a life sentence for the murder of three children - Alice da Silva Aguiar, 9, Bebe King, 6, and Elsie Dot Stancombe, 7 - and attempted murder of eight other children and two adults. On Thursday, Rudakubana was moved from HMP Belmarsh to a high-security psychiatric hospital in Berkshire.

stories.title: Secret gambling, lavish lifestyle: Supreme Court lawyer Thomas Goldstein faces sentencing
stories.summary: He won and lost tens of millions of dollars while gambling, evaded taxes and lied to mortgage lenders A federal jury found SCOTUS blog co-founder Thomas Goldstein guilty of 12 of 16 counts after a six-week trial in Greenbelt. He is expected to be sentenced Friday. (ALEX BRANDON/AP) In addition to facing prison time, his marriage broke up, and he stepped away from the boutique firm he founded. His reputation as a stellar lawyer is in ruins.

stories.title: Julia Roberts’ Loose ‘Vacation Pants’ Are Still a Summer Must-Have — Copy the Look for $20
stories.summary: About a year after cleaning out my closet and donating a bunch of clothes, I realized something: everything leftover felt kind of boring, and I needed to add more whimsical items, STAT. Perhaps the bright yellow pants I once owned were no longer a fit, but I often think about Julia Roberts' breezy striped bottoms, wishing the same style sat in my wardrobe. The star's bold, black-and-white pick — which, yes, she wore way back in 1993 — has remained on my mind for years, and much like her iconic polka-dot dress from Pretty Woman, the easy-yet-luxe design still feels like a summer staple.

stories.title: Jürgen Klopp confirmed as new Germany head coach for Euro 2028
stories.summary: Jürgen Klopp is to return to the dugout after being appointed head coach of the Germany men’s national side until 2030. The 59-year-old takes over from Julian Nagelsmann in the job after Germany’s humbling last-32 defeat by Paraguay at the World Cup . Klopp has been away from frontline management since leaving Liverpool at the end of the 2023-24 season, when he cited fatigue as his principal reason for stepping away after a hugely successful nine-season spell in which he win the Premier League and Champions League, among other trophies. Klopp was made Red Bull’s head of global soccer in October 2024, working in the background for the multiclub organisation.

stories.title: Darren Purse takes over at Sligo Rovers
stories.summary: Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 09:22 Sligo Rovers have appointed Darren Purse as manager Former Premier League defender Darren Purse has been appointed as the new Sligo Rovers manager on an 18-month contract. Purse, who played for two seasons with Birmingham City in the English top flight, arrives at the Showgrounds from Cardiff City, where he spent almost five years working with the club's Under-23 and U21 sides, running their loan programme and serving a spell as assistant manager of the first team. He replaces John Russell, who ended an eight-year spell with the Connacht club to take over at Shelbourne earlier this month.

stories.title: Steve LePore | Coffee Bars or Compassion?
stories.summary: Steve LePore | Coffee Bars or Compassion? An open letter to the pastors of Santa Clarita: “If a brother or sister is poorly clothed and lacking in daily food, and one of you says, ‘Go in peace, be warmed and filled,’ without giving them the things needed for the body, what good is that? ” — James 2:15-16. Here’s a question I can’t stop asking. What are your churches doing this week for the people in Santa Clarita who are too afraid to leave their homes? I’m not talking about politics. I’m not talking about immigration policy.

stories.title: Kerry election candidate jailed for contempt after ‘manifestly unfair’ hearing, High Court rules
stories.summary: A judge in Kerry conducted a “manifestly unfair” hearing in which he sent independent election candidate Michelle Keane to prison for contempt of court over an alleged refusal to take down a social media post about a garda, the High Court said. Keane is to seek an award of damages over her jailing. Judge Brian Cregan made the comments as he overturned a finding of contempt by Judge James O’Donohoe in Listowel Circuit Court on December 10th, 2024, when he jailed Keane after she allegedly refused to give an undertaking not to remove the post.

stories.title: Iran, Houthis drive $49 oil price hike; demand cuts trim $10
stories.summary: Iran and Houthi forces have reportedly contributed to a $49 increase in oil prices, while concurrent demand cuts have resulted in a $10 decrease, according to ZeroHedge. This development occurs against a backdrop of fluctuating oil prices due to Middle Eastern geopolitical tensions, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz. The Brent crude benchmark, which was around $76–$77 in early July 2026, has been subject to sharp swings influenced by fears of disruptions in oil flow. The International Energy Agency (IEA) had previously noted a decline in oil prices in June due to a ceasefire that helped stabilize supply.

stories.title: Can Jedd Fisch, Washington Huskies Answer KEY DEPTH QUESTIONS During Fall Camp?
stories.summary: Can Jedd Fisch, Washington Huskies Answer KEY DEPTH QUESTIONS During Fall Camp? To stream KVUE on your phone, you need the KVUE app. Download the KVUE app More Videos Next up in 5 Example video title will go here for this video Next up in 5 Example video title will go here for this video The Washington Huskies are looking for players to step into key rotational roles at several positions.

stories.title: Man Utd handed Tchouameni boost - Friday's gossip
stories.summary: Manchester United are set to sign France midfielder Aurelien Tchouameni from Real Madrid for a reported fee around £60 million. Arsenal are targeting England defenders John Stones and Ezri Konsa as injury concerns mount. Chelsea are close to finalising a deal for Bosnia-Herzegovina teenager Kerim Alajbegovic.

stories.title: Luxbox Acquires Documentary ‘Life of Jorge Luis Borges’ Ahead of Venice Premiere (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Luxbox Films has acquired the world sales rights to Mariano Llinás ‘ epic five-and-a-half -hour documentary “Life of Jorge Luis Borges,” which will world premiere at the Venice Film Festival in the Open – Non-Fiction section. The film forms a biography of Argentine writer and poet Jorge Luis Borges, working from an exhaustive review of the papers he left, the books he read, and the places he went to. The film is also “an attempt to decipher a very peculiar life: someone who lived like a hidden, underground warrior, whose cause was literature, literature conceived as a kingdom and as a landscape,” according to a statement.

stories.title: Guardiola turns down Italy manager job
stories.summary: Former Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola turns down the chance to coach Italy. Pep Guardiola has turned down the chance to coach the Italian national side. Earlier this week, Italian Football Federation (FIGC) president Giovanni Malago said talks had been held with Guardiola, who left Manchester City this summer after 10 years, and that "exceptions" could be made for his salary. On Thursday, FIGC technical director Paolo Maldini said Italy first approached their former player Carlo Ancelotti, who is currently in charge of Brazil.

stories.title: UK mortgage rates rise to highest level for a month
stories.summary: UK mortgage rates have risen to their highest level in a month, reversing recent declines and leaving borrowers facing renewed uncertainty. Lenders have temporarily withdrawn 100 deals as they reassess pricing, while experts advise locking in current offers early or consulting brokers for better options. Analysts warn the trend may continue, with fixed rates expected to rise further in the coming months.

stories.title: Jon Bon Jovi Ends Madison Square Garden Show Early Due to Sinus Infection: ‘We’ll Figure Out How to Reschedule’
stories.summary: Bon Jovi ‘s penultimate show of their Madison Square Garden residency came to an early end on Thursday night after frontman Jon Bon Jovi revealed he was suffering from a sinus infection. About 90 minutes into the set, Bon Jovi told the crowd that he would have to stop the show as he wasn’t feeling his best. “I haven’t talked to anybody about anything, but don’t throw away your ticket stubs,” Bon Jovi said, according to fan video . “I’m going to figure something out, OK? Just hold onto them, we’ll figure out how to reschedule. But I’m going to have to cool it for the night. ” He ended the show by saying, “I’ll see you again soon.

stories.title: England amputee team's World Cup dream on hold
stories.summary: England's amputee football team faces missing the World Cup in Mexico after failing to secure £50,000 in funding. Former coach Scott Rogers called the potential exclusion "criminal" as the team, reigning Nations League champions, relies on donations and volunteers. The Football Association previously offered partial funding after observing the team at the 2022 World Cup.

stories.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
stories.summary: It comes as Iran condemned the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages. Iran says it has carried out drone strikes on US military facilities and bases across the Middle East in retaliation for the 13th consecutive night of American attacks on the country. Explosions were reported along Iran's Gulf coast, and in western, northern and central areas. Four people were killed and five injured in an attack in the south-west, Iranian state media said. Iran later announced strikes on US-allied Kuwait, while sirens sounded in Bahrain and Jordan, and flights were suspended at a major Iraqi airport after a reported drone crash.

stories.title: Microsoft pressures LG into killing unwanted McAfee ads
stories.summary: Microsoft has forced LG to halt McAfee trial pop-ups that were automatically installing on Windows 11 PCs via LG monitor drivers. LG agreed to disable the pop-ups immediately after Microsoft’s intervention, following widespread complaints about unwanted bloatware installations. The tech giant says it will continue working with partners to improve user control over such software installations.

stories.title: France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
stories.summary: French authorities have ordered the full evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula in southwest France, with 40,000 people fleeing by boat as wildfires consume 8,700 hectares. Around 80 homes have been damaged, 50 of them destroyed, as firefighters battle blazes that began Tuesday near Saumos. Spain has also declared a national emergency amid multiple wildfires near Madrid.

stories.title: TV tonight: the great pandemic drama you might have missed
stories.summary: Station Eleven 9pm, Sky Atlantic A great – and somehow hopeful – apocalyptic drama, which deserved more love when it first streamed in 2022. (Perhaps the story of a pandemic wiping out 99% of the world was too close to home. ) It’s set across two timelines, with young actor Kirsten (Matilda Lawler) being looked after by writer Jeevan (Himesh Patel) when the killer flu strikes; 20 years later (now played by Mackenzie Davis) she tours with the Travelling Symphony. Hollie Richardson Prog Rock at the Proms 7pm, BBC Four It was probably only a matter of time before this once scorned but now largely rehabilitated rock subgenre got its Albert Hall moment.

stories.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
stories.summary: Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has ruled out introducing rent controls in England, saying the Renters' Rights Act already addresses market pressures. She noted that past rent freezes in parts of the UK did not consistently lower rents. Scotland plans to introduce targeted rent controls by 2027.

stories.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: The US has imposed new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on goods from 60 countries, including the UK, China and the EU, covering nearly all American imports. The move replaces an expiring 10% levy and targets nations accused of failing to address forced labour. The White House says the duties aim to combat trade distortions and human rights abuses.

stories.title: Unlike Joe Duffy, Kieran Cuddihy doesn’t ramp up the drama on Liveline
stories.summary: For any broadcaster, the ability to see both sides of the story is a crucial skill, but it’s mandatory on a show such as Liveline ( RTÉ Radio 1 , weekdays), where everyone basically thinks they’re in the right. Even so, Kieran Cuddihy goes above and beyond in his duty as host of the phone-in show, going one step farther by contradicting his own argument even as he makes it. On Tuesday, as callers debate whether helmets and high-vis gear should be compulsory for cyclists , Cuddihy suggests that legislating on such matters is meaningless without proper enforcement. As an example, he points to the legal requirement for lights on bikes after dark. “It’s not enforced.

stories.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
stories.summary: A woman’s wedding planning triggered a dangerous cycle of crash dieting and binge eating after years of casual weightlifting. She dropped to her lowest weight in a decade before realizing the extreme measures were harming her health. Doctors now warn against such drastic measures ahead of major life events.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
stories.summary: Spain midfielder Gavi says Argentina’s Leandro Paredes should not face suspension for his role in a post-match altercation after Spain’s 1-0 World Cup final win. FIFA is reviewing the incident involving Paredes, Molina, Almada and assistant coach Ayala, with possible suspensions or fines for those involved. Gavi told Spanish radio the aggression was part of football’s physical nature and argued Paredes should have been sent off during the game instead.

stories.title: ICC member states decide fate of embattled prosecutor Karim Khan
stories.summary: In May 2025, Khan had stepped back from his duties to fight the allegations made by one of his female colleagues, after an inquiry was launched the previous year. The 56-year-old Briton was then suspended last month by a key governing body of the ICC, the 21-member bureau of the Assembly of State Parties (ASP), which called Friday's meeting at UN headquarters in New York. At that time, the bureau said the suspension was "not an indication of the final outcome" in the matter. The decision on whether to relieve Khan of his duties is now subject to a secret ballot by the 125 countries party to the International Criminal Court during a closed-door session. An absolute majority carries the vote.

stories.title: ‘My neighbour raped me in my own home. Releasing him early would be beyond the pale’
stories.summary: Last Friday, an unexpected call from the Parole Board made Angela Wright’s* blood boil. Barely three years since her neighbour was convicted of her rape, she was told that he would be freed after serving less than half his sentence through the Government’s early release scheme – the plan to release an estimated 2,500 murderers and rapists from September due to prison overcrowding. She spent the best part of a week “in turmoil” until Thursday afternoon when, following backlash over the planned early release of two of the three killers of Pc Andrew Harper, the scheme was temporarily “halted” .

stories.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
stories.summary: A South Korean court has ordered SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won to pay his ex-wife Roh Soh-yeong $644 million in what local media calls the "divorce of the century." The final award of 944 billion won is lower than the initial 1.38 trillion won demanded in 2024 after Roh, daughter of a former president, sought a share of Chey’s assets following his admission of infidelity. The ruling follows over a decade of legal battles over the division of assets in their 35-year marriage.

stories.title: Why does Zapatero reappear now?
stories.summary: Why does Zapatero reappear now? This Thursday is an anniversary day. It has been exactly 26 years since José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero was elected Secretary General of the PSOE. At that time, he could not have imagined that a quarter of a century later he would be sitting on the set of Mañaneros on TVE giving explanations for two serious criminal cases: one for alleged influence peddling for the Plus Ultra bailout and another for alleged smuggling of millionaire jewelry found in the safe of his office. Why has it appeared now? In fact, the explanations, according to what he himself had announced, should have arrived earlier.

stories.title: Four in a row: Sounders hit new low
stories.summary: The Seattle Sounders suffered their fourth straight MLS loss, falling 3–1 to bottom-placed Austin FC. It’s their worst streak since 2013, with the team outscored 11–2 over the four games. Sounders defender Jackson Ragen was sent off late as Austin sealed the win with two stoppage-time goals.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: As Canada Opens Gordie Howe International Bridge, Americans Are Told to Stay Home
stories.summary: After a delay ordered by President Trump and new tariffs against Canada, the bridge between Windsor, Ontario, and Detroit opens in “underwhelming” fashion on Friday.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: Young people in coastal towns across England and Wales face growing isolation and limited opportunities, with youth service funding down 73% in 12 years and nearly a million aged 16 to 24 not in work, education or training. A year-long Guardian project highlights how decades of underinvestment and negative stereotypes have left many feeling abandoned by the sea they call home. The series will continue exploring these challenges and potential solutions.

stories.title: Democrats Prepare for Possible Election Clashes With Federal Government
stories.summary: Top officials overseeing state elections and law enforcement are stepping up plans to prepare for what they worry could be interference in the midterms.

stories.title: Iran War and Trump’s Tariffs Threaten a Resilient US Economy
stories.summary: As renewed fighting with Iran sends oil and gas prices soaring, President Trump has imposed new global tariffs that could push up prices further.

stories.title: An A.I. Music F.A.Q.: Can I Remix Madonna? Is This All Legal?
stories.summary: Advances in A.I. are making it possible to create all kinds of music from scratch, but they also raise questions about what is legal and who will actually listen.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
stories.summary: Protest leaders from India’s Cockroach Janta Party will meet government officials on Friday as nationwide demonstrations demanding the education minister’s resignation enter their fourth week. The talks follow activist Sonam Wangchuk’s 26-day hunger strike, which ended last night after he was hospitalized during a police crackdown that left scores injured. Prime Minister Narendra Modi has pledged stricter laws on exam paper leaks, but protesters say the minister must step down first.

stories.title: ‘Jumbo’ Sequel, ‘Queen of Malacca’ Franchise Plans on the Cards as Indonesia’s Visinema Eyes Expansion (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Indonesia’s Visinema is set to release the fantasy epic Queen of Malacca in early 2027. The sequel to Jumbo is in post-production and has sparked interest from distributors in China and Thailand.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron has been spotted in Somerset, marking its first confirmed UK sighting in years. The bird was filmed at the National Trust’s Holnicote Estate, where conservationists have restored freshwater habitats to support wildlife. Conservationists say the sighting highlights the success of wetland restoration efforts in reversing habitat loss.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: England’s rare chalk streams—home to over 80% of the world’s total—face growing pressure from prolonged drought, threatening wildlife. Winchester Science Centre in Hampshire has opened a new play area to teach children about these unique habitats, linking water flow, temperature and oxygen to survival. The exhibit aims to inspire lifelong conservation interest as groups like BBOWT push for stronger protections.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study found replacing gas stoves with electric cut hospital visits and missed school or work by 70% for poorly controlled asthma patients. The project, involving 1,200 Cleveland-area households, was halted early in December 2024 after funding ended. Researchers reported asthma improvements matching or exceeding those from common medications.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

offbeat.title: Elon Musk brutal reply to journalist’s ‘people loathe you’ remark wins internet
offbeat.summary: Elon Musk brutal reply to journalist’s ‘people loathe you’ remark wins internet Elon Musk’s recent interview with The Economist Editor-in-Chief has become the talk of the town and the most talked-about moment was SpaceX's savage reply to her question. The fiery exchange between Musk and Zanny Minton Beddoes stemmed from her remarks, claiming that many people loathe the 54-year-old billionaire. During the interview, Minton argued about Musk’s growing involvement in political affairs specifically outside the US, stating, “You don't even live there. ” She remarked that people felt uncomfortable with the “world’s richest man’s” involvement in politics though he does not live here.

offbeat.summary: Two stages, two completely different approaches to one of cycling’s most iconic climbs, and a spectacular finale in the battle for the podium. Stage 19: The classic climb with 21 hairpin bends Friday’s Stage 19 features the legendary ascent from Bourg-d’Oisans to Alpe d’Huez. At just under 128 kilometres, it is the shortest mass-start stage of this year’s Tour, but don’t let the distance fool you. With around 3,500 metres of climbing, the riders face a demanding day capped by one of cycling’s most famous finishes. The 16-kilometre climb, with its iconic 21 hairpin bends, is synonymous with Tour de France history.

offbeat.title: View Interior Photos of the 2027 Maserati Grecale Modena
offbeat.summary: Read the Full Story | View Exterior PhotosThe 2027 Maserati Grecale features a few tweaks to its cabin, but the model year's most notable changes happen elsewhere in the vehicle. In base-trim form, the Grecale's interior quality outpaces the gas-powered Porsche Macan's. We're not the biggest fans of that slanted touch panel for the climate controls, though. The seats, however, are marvelous. Look, ma—real buttons! The mid-level Modena trim picks up a 14-speaker Sonus Faber sound system. Keep going to check out even more pictures of the 2027 Maserati Grecale's interior.

offbeat.title: Ukraine ‘priority’ for Ireland during EU presidency, Taoiseach says
offbeat.summary: The Irish premier has said it was “very important” to go to Ukraine early in Ireland’s EU presidency as a demonstration of solidarity. Micheál Martin was speaking to reporters in the country’s capital, Kyiv , on Thursday. Asked if there was a danger the EU’s focus would drift because of the war in the Middle East, he said “we have too many wars in the world”, adding that conflict has had “a very, very negative impact” there and on the wider world. The Taoiseach said it was “very, very important to come out here early” during Ireland’s six-month presidency of the Council of the European Union to make clear to Ukraine “they are a priority in terms of the Irish presidency”.

offbeat.title: Aughinish Alumina investigation makes ‘no adverse findings’ against Co Limerick plant
offbeat.summary: An investigation into Aughinish Alumina and the company’s export of raw material to Russia has made “no adverse findings” against the Co Limerick plant, Minister for Enterprise Peter Burke has said. In a statement setting out the findings of the inquiry, Burke said there was “no information of a sufficient evidentiary level” found to confirm that alumina produced by the Russian-owned plant was later being used to supply Russian arms manufacturers making missiles and weapons for Moscow’s war in Ukraine.

His reputation as a stellar lawyer is in ruins.

The 59-year-old takes over from Julian Nagelsmann in the job after Germany’s humbling last-32 defeat by Paraguay at the World Cup . offbeat.title: Darren Purse takes over at Sligo Rovers
offbeat.summary: Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 09:22 Sligo Rovers have appointed Darren Purse as manager Former Premier League defender Darren Purse has been appointed as the new Sligo Rovers manager on an 18-month contract. Purse, who played for two seasons with Birmingham City in the English top flight, arrives at the Showgrounds from Cardiff City, where he spent almost five years working with the club's Under-23 and U21 sides, running their loan programme and serving a spell as assistant manager of the first team. He replaces John Russell, who ended an eight-year spell with the Connacht club to take over at Shelbourne earlier this month.

offbeat.title: Steve LePore | Coffee Bars or Compassion?
offbeat.summary: Steve LePore | Coffee Bars or Compassion? An open letter to the pastors of Santa Clarita: “If a brother or sister is poorly clothed and lacking in daily food, and one of you says, ‘Go in peace, be warmed and filled,’ without giving them the things needed for the body, what good is that? ” — James 2:15-16. Here’s a question I can’t stop asking. What are your churches doing this week for the people in Santa Clarita who are too afraid to leave their homes? I’m not talking about politics. I’m not talking about immigration policy.

offbeat.title: Kerry election candidate jailed for contempt after ‘manifestly unfair’ hearing, High Court rules
offbeat.summary: A judge in Kerry conducted a “manifestly unfair” hearing in which he sent independent election candidate Michelle Keane to prison for contempt of court over an alleged refusal to take down a social media post about a garda, the High Court said. Keane is to seek an award of damages over her jailing. Judge Brian Cregan made the comments as he overturned a finding of contempt by Judge James O’Donohoe in Listowel Circuit Court on December 10th, 2024, when he jailed Keane after she allegedly refused to give an undertaking not to remove the post.

offbeat.title: Iran, Houthis drive $49 oil price hike; demand cuts trim $10
offbeat.summary: Iran and Houthi forces have reportedly contributed to a $49 increase in oil prices, while concurrent demand cuts have resulted in a $10 decrease, according to ZeroHedge. This development occurs against a backdrop of fluctuating oil prices due to Middle Eastern geopolitical tensions, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz. The Brent crude benchmark, which was around $76–$77 in early July 2026, has been subject to sharp swings influenced by fears of disruptions in oil flow. The International Energy Agency (IEA) had previously noted a decline in oil prices in June due to a ceasefire that helped stabilize supply.

offbeat.title: Can Jedd Fisch, Washington Huskies Answer KEY DEPTH QUESTIONS During Fall Camp?
offbeat.summary: Can Jedd Fisch, Washington Huskies Answer KEY DEPTH QUESTIONS During Fall Camp? To stream KVUE on your phone, you need the KVUE app. Download the KVUE app More Videos Next up in 5 Example video title will go here for this video Next up in 5 Example video title will go here for this video The Washington Huskies are looking for players to step into key rotational roles at several positions.

offbeat.title: TV tonight: the great pandemic drama you might have missed
offbeat.summary: Station Eleven 9pm, Sky Atlantic A great – and somehow hopeful – apocalyptic drama, which deserved more love when it first streamed in 2022. (Perhaps the story of a pandemic wiping out 99% of the world was too close to home. ) It’s set across two timelines, with young actor Kirsten (Matilda Lawler) being looked after by writer Jeevan (Himesh Patel) when the killer flu strikes; 20 years later (now played by Mackenzie Davis) she tours with the Travelling Symphony. Hollie Richardson Prog Rock at the Proms 7pm, BBC Four It was probably only a matter of time before this once scorned but now largely rehabilitated rock subgenre got its Albert Hall moment.

offbeat.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
offbeat.summary: The US has imposed new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on goods from 60 countries, including the UK, China and the EU, covering nearly all American imports. The move replaces an expiring 10% levy and targets nations accused of failing to address forced labour. The White House says the duties aim to combat trade distortions and human rights abuses.

offbeat.title: Unlike Joe Duffy, Kieran Cuddihy doesn’t ramp up the drama on Liveline
offbeat.summary: For any broadcaster, the ability to see both sides of the story is a crucial skill, but it’s mandatory on a show such as Liveline ( RTÉ Radio 1 , weekdays), where everyone basically thinks they’re in the right. Even so, Kieran Cuddihy goes above and beyond in his duty as host of the phone-in show, going one step farther by contradicting his own argument even as he makes it. On Tuesday, as callers debate whether helmets and high-vis gear should be compulsory for cyclists , Cuddihy suggests that legislating on such matters is meaningless without proper enforcement. As an example, he points to the legal requirement for lights on bikes after dark. “It’s not enforced.

offbeat.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
offbeat.summary: Spain midfielder Gavi says Argentina’s Leandro Paredes should not face suspension for his role in a post-match altercation after Spain’s 1-0 World Cup final win. FIFA is reviewing the incident involving Paredes, Molina, Almada and assistant coach Ayala, with possible suspensions or fines for those involved. Gavi told Spanish radio the aggression was part of football’s physical nature and argued Paredes should have been sent off during the game instead.

offbeat.title: ICC member states decide fate of embattled prosecutor Karim Khan
offbeat.summary: In May 2025, Khan had stepped back from his duties to fight the allegations made by one of his female colleagues, after an inquiry was launched the previous year. The 56-year-old Briton was then suspended last month by a key governing body of the ICC, the 21-member bureau of the Assembly of State Parties (ASP), which called Friday's meeting at UN headquarters in New York. At that time, the bureau said the suspension was "not an indication of the final outcome" in the matter. The decision on whether to relieve Khan of his duties is now subject to a secret ballot by the 125 countries party to the International Criminal Court during a closed-door session. An absolute majority carries the vote.

offbeat.title: ‘My neighbour raped me in my own home. Releasing him early would be beyond the pale’
offbeat.summary: Last Friday, an unexpected call from the Parole Board made Angela Wright’s* blood boil. Barely three years since her neighbour was convicted of her rape, she was told that he would be freed after serving less than half his sentence through the Government’s early release scheme – the plan to release an estimated 2,500 murderers and rapists from September due to prison overcrowding. She spent the best part of a week “in turmoil” until Thursday afternoon when, following backlash over the planned early release of two of the three killers of Pc Andrew Harper, the scheme was temporarily “halted” .

offbeat.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
offbeat.summary: A South Korean court has ordered SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won to pay his ex-wife Roh Soh-yeong $644 million in what local media calls the "divorce of the century." The final award of 944 billion won is lower than the initial 1.38 trillion won demanded in 2024 after Roh, daughter of a former president, sought a share of Chey’s assets following his admission of infidelity. The ruling follows over a decade of legal battles over the division of assets in their 35-year marriage.

offbeat.title: Why does Zapatero reappear now?
offbeat.summary: Why does Zapatero reappear now? This Thursday is an anniversary day. It has been exactly 26 years since José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero was elected Secretary General of the PSOE. At that time, he could not have imagined that a quarter of a century later he would be sitting on the set of Mañaneros on TVE giving explanations for two serious criminal cases: one for alleged influence peddling for the Plus Ultra bailout and another for alleged smuggling of millionaire jewelry found in the safe of his office. Why has it appeared now? In fact, the explanations, according to what he himself had announced, should have arrived earlier.

offbeat.title: Four in a row: Sounders hit new low
offbeat.summary: The Seattle Sounders suffered their fourth straight MLS loss, falling 3–1 to bottom-placed Austin FC. It’s their worst streak since 2013, with the team outscored 11–2 over the four games. Sounders defender Jackson Ragen was sent off late as Austin sealed the win with two stoppage-time goals.

offbeat.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
offbeat.summary: Protest leaders from India’s Cockroach Janta Party will meet government officials on Friday as nationwide demonstrations demanding the education minister’s resignation enter their fourth week. The talks follow activist Sonam Wangchuk’s 26-day hunger strike, which ended last night after he was hospitalized during a police crackdown that left scores injured. Prime Minister Narendra Modi has pledged stricter laws on exam paper leaks, but protesters say the minister must step down first.

sports.title: Man Utd handed Tchouameni boost - Friday's gossip
sports.summary: Manchester United are set to sign France midfielder Aurelien Tchouameni from Real Madrid for a reported fee around £60 million. Arsenal are targeting England defenders John Stones and Ezri Konsa as injury concerns mount. Chelsea are close to finalising a deal for Bosnia-Herzegovina teenager Kerim Alajbegovic.

sports.title: Guardiola turns down Italy manager job
sports.summary: Former Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola turns down the chance to coach Italy. Pep Guardiola has turned down the chance to coach the Italian national side. Earlier this week, Italian Football Federation (FIGC) president Giovanni Malago said talks had been held with Guardiola, who left Manchester City this summer after 10 years, and that "exceptions" could be made for his salary. On Thursday, FIGC technical director Paolo Maldini said Italy first approached their former player Carlo Ancelotti, who is currently in charge of Brazil.

sports.title: England amputee team's World Cup dream on hold
sports.summary: England's amputee football team faces missing the World Cup in Mexico after failing to secure £50,000 in funding. Former coach Scott Rogers called the potential exclusion "criminal" as the team, reigning Nations League champions, relies on donations and volunteers. The Football Association previously offered partial funding after observing the team at the 2022 World Cup.

entertainment.title: Luxbox Acquires Documentary ‘Life of Jorge Luis Borges’ Ahead of Venice Premiere (EXCLUSIVE)
entertainment.summary: Luxbox Films has acquired the world sales rights to Mariano Llinás ‘ epic five-and-a-half -hour documentary “Life of Jorge Luis Borges,” which will world premiere at the Venice Film Festival in the Open – Non-Fiction section. The film forms a biography of Argentine writer and poet Jorge Luis Borges, working from an exhaustive review of the papers he left, the books he read, and the places he went to. The film is also “an attempt to decipher a very peculiar life: someone who lived like a hidden, underground warrior, whose cause was literature, literature conceived as a kingdom and as a landscape,” according to a statement.

entertainment.title: Jon Bon Jovi Ends Madison Square Garden Show Early Due to Sinus Infection: ‘We’ll Figure Out How to Reschedule’
entertainment.summary: Bon Jovi ‘s penultimate show of their Madison Square Garden residency came to an early end on Thursday night after frontman Jon Bon Jovi revealed he was suffering from a sinus infection. About 90 minutes into the set, Bon Jovi told the crowd that he would have to stop the show as he wasn’t feeling his best. “I haven’t talked to anybody about anything, but don’t throw away your ticket stubs,” Bon Jovi said, according to fan video . “I’m going to figure something out, OK? Just hold onto them, we’ll figure out how to reschedule. But I’m going to have to cool it for the night. ” He ended the show by saying, “I’ll see you again soon.

entertainment.title: ‘Jumbo’ Sequel, ‘Queen of Malacca’ Franchise Plans on the Cards as Indonesia’s Visinema Eyes Expansion (EXCLUSIVE)
entertainment.summary: Indonesia’s Visinema is set to release the fantasy epic Queen of Malacca in early 2027. The sequel to Jumbo is in post-production and has sparked interest from distributors in China and Thailand.

crisis.title: UK mortgage rates rise to highest level for a month
crisis.summary: UK mortgage rates have risen to their highest level in a month, reversing recent declines and leaving borrowers facing renewed uncertainty. Lenders have temporarily withdrawn 100 deals as they reassess pricing, while experts advise locking in current offers early or consulting brokers for better options. Analysts warn the trend may continue, with fixed rates expected to rise further in the coming months.

crisis.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
crisis.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

crisis.title: Iran War and Trump’s Tariffs Threaten a Resilient US Economy
crisis.summary: As renewed fighting with Iran sends oil and gas prices soaring, President Trump has imposed new global tariffs that could push up prices further.

breaking.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
breaking.summary: It comes as Iran condemned the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages. Iran says it has carried out drone strikes on US military facilities and bases across the Middle East in retaliation for the 13th consecutive night of American attacks on the country. Explosions were reported along Iran's Gulf coast, and in western, northern and central areas. Four people were killed and five injured in an attack in the south-west, Iranian state media said. Iran later announced strikes on US-allied Kuwait, while sirens sounded in Bahrain and Jordan, and flights were suspended at a major Iraqi airport after a reported drone crash.

breaking.title: France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
breaking.summary: French authorities have ordered the full evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula in southwest France, with 40,000 people fleeing by boat as wildfires consume 8,700 hectares. Around 80 homes have been damaged, 50 of them destroyed, as firefighters battle blazes that began Tuesday near Saumos. Spain has also declared a national emergency amid multiple wildfires near Madrid.

breaking.title: As Canada Opens Gordie Howe International Bridge, Americans Are Told to Stay Home
breaking.summary: After a delay ordered by President Trump and new tariffs against Canada, the bridge between Windsor, Ontario, and Detroit opens in “underwhelming” fashion on Friday.

tech.title: Microsoft pressures LG into killing unwanted McAfee ads
tech.summary: Microsoft has forced LG to halt McAfee trial pop-ups that were automatically installing on Windows 11 PCs via LG monitor drivers. LG agreed to disable the pop-ups immediately after Microsoft’s intervention, following widespread complaints about unwanted bloatware installations. The tech giant says it will continue working with partners to improve user control over such software installations.

tech.title: An A.I. Music F.A.Q.: Can I Remix Madonna? Is This All Legal?
tech.summary: Advances in A.I. are making it possible to create all kinds of music from scratch, but they also raise questions about what is legal and who will actually listen.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

politics.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
politics.summary: Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has ruled out introducing rent controls in England, saying the Renters' Rights Act already addresses market pressures. She noted that past rent freezes in parts of the UK did not consistently lower rents. Scotland plans to introduce targeted rent controls by 2027.

politics.title: Democrats Prepare for Possible Election Clashes With Federal Government
politics.summary: Top officials overseeing state elections and law enforcement are stepping up plans to prepare for what they worry could be interference in the midterms.

health.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
health.summary: A woman’s wedding planning triggered a dangerous cycle of crash dieting and binge eating after years of casual weightlifting. She dropped to her lowest weight in a decade before realizing the extreme measures were harming her health. Doctors now warn against such drastic measures ahead of major life events.

health.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
health.summary: Young people in coastal towns across England and Wales face growing isolation and limited opportunities, with youth service funding down 73% in 12 years and nearly a million aged 16 to 24 not in work, education or training. A year-long Guardian project highlights how decades of underinvestment and negative stereotypes have left many feeling abandoned by the sea they call home. The series will continue exploring these challenges and potential solutions.

health.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
health.summary: A US study found replacing gas stoves with electric cut hospital visits and missed school or work by 70% for poorly controlled asthma patients. The project, involving 1,200 Cleveland-area households, was halted early in December 2024 after funding ended. Researchers reported asthma improvements matching or exceeding those from common medications.

facts.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
facts.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron has been spotted in Somerset, marking its first confirmed UK sighting in years. The bird was filmed at the National Trust’s Holnicote Estate, where conservationists have restored freshwater habitats to support wildlife. Conservationists say the sighting highlights the success of wetland restoration efforts in reversing habitat loss.

facts.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
facts.summary: England’s rare chalk streams—home to over 80% of the world’s total—face growing pressure from prolonged drought, threatening wildlife. Winchester Science Centre in Hampshire has opened a new play area to teach children about these unique habitats, linking water flow, temperature and oxygen to survival. The exhibit aims to inspire lifelong conservation interest as groups like BBOWT push for stronger protections.
